extra.reason: Semantic Runtime section added to CLAUDE.md with principles and crate-responsibility table
kopitiam-ontology implemented: EntityId/RelationshipId, EntityKind, Entity, RelationshipKind, Relationship, all serde-serializable with unit tests. Builds and tests pass in release mode.

Implemented CargoMetadataProvider (real cargo_metadata integration, tested against this workspace), RustAnalyzerProvider (real LSP client over stdio, tested against installed rust-analyzer, includes indexing-progress wait), RustdocProvider (real cargo +nightly rustdoc invocation, gracefully degrades to empty on this stable-only toolchain, verified via test). All three implement KnowledgeProvider. Dropped lsp-types dependency in favor of hand-rolled minimal JSON-RPC to keep footprint small.

Implemented SemanticGraph: in-memory entity/relationship store with insert/extend, kind-filtered queries, and bidirectional relationship traversal. Serde-serializable for later persistence via kopitiam-index without a hard dependency on it. Unit tests cover insertion, traversal, and JSON round-trip.

Implemented rename + code actions: LspClient gained UTF-8 position-encoding negotiation, textDocument/rename, textDocument/codeAction, workspace/executeCommand, and a generic handler for server-initiated requests (answers workspace/applyEdit by writing to disk, acks registerCapability/workDoneProgress/create). New kopitiam_semantic::edit module applies WorkspaceEdits (documentChanges and legacy changes forms) via a pure compute-then-write split, with unified diff preview via . New RustAnalyzerSession wraps this in a path-based API. Wired into apps/cli as 'rename' (--apply gate, diff preview by default) and 'code-actions' (list then --apply <index>) subcommands. Verified end-to-end with a real rename against this repo (rust-analyzer correctly updated a cross-reference in a test module); confirmed preview mode left the file untouched on disk.

Implemented Store: redb-backed (pure Rust, no C dep) key-value persistence rooted at a project-relative .kopitiam directory -- cross-platform by construction since it is a plain relative path with no OS-specific lookup code, works unmodified on Linux/macOS/Windows/Android. put/get/delete plus put_json/get_json typed helpers. Tests cover round-trip, JSON round-trip, directory creation, and persistence across reopening (separate Database instances).

Implemented ProjectState: current_task, capped working_set (LRU-ish touch/evict), updated_at timestamp; load/save through kopitiam-index's Store under a fixed key. Tests cover default-on-first-load, save/load round trip, touch dedup-and-reorder, and working-set capacity eviction.

Wired kopitiam-ontology/kopitiam-semantic/kopitiam-knowledge/kopitiam-index/kopitiam-workspace into apps/cli as real subcommands: scan (providers -> graph, persists a working-set touch), status (reads persisted ProjectState back), rename and code-actions (rust-analyzer write path). All verified against this real repo, including a live rename that correctly updated a cross-reference. Generous rustdoc added throughout apps/cli per instruction. Remaining engines (kopitiam-search, kopitiam-workflow, kopitiam-translation, kopitiam-ai) still open as separate tasks for future CLI wiring.
Completed as part of this session; see description for full detail.
ModelAdapter trait + Message/CompletionRequest/CompletionResponse types + EchoAdapter stub implemented, compiles in release mode with passing tests
SearchIndex (create_in_dir/open_in_dir/add_entity/commit/search) implemented over tantivy 0.26, indexing kopitiam-ontology Entity records; tempdir index/search round-trip test passes
LanguageAdapter trait + TranslationState (register/mark_translated/mark_verified/mark_failed, persisted via kopitiam-index) implemented with unit tests
kopitiam-workflow crate implemented: ContextBuilder (no kopitiam-ai dep), Workflow trait + WorkflowKind (8 named workflows), run_workflow pipeline; wired into apps/cli as 'kopitiam plan'

All child crates (ontology, semantic, knowledge, index, search, workspace, workflow, translation, ai) now exist with real minimal implementations, compile in release mode, and are wired into apps/cli. CLAUDE.md documents the architecture.

Cloned all 10 reference repos (--depth 1) into crates/kopitiam-ai/vendor/, gitignored; wrote docs/ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS.md with license table, AGPLv3 compatibility notes, and the clean-room implementation principle.

Replaced word-count recovery ratio with non-whitespace CHARACTER count on both sides, stripping renderer-added markdown scaffolding (heading #, list markers, table pipes, fences) and soft wrap-hyphens. Char count is invariant to re-tokenization, which was the root cause of false FAILs. Verified on the real syllabus PDF: old word ratio 34% (false FAIL) -> new char ratio 100.0% PASS. Old word ratio retained as informational. Threshold raised 0.95 -> 0.98. Rustdoc is explicit that this detects content LOSS only, not corruption/misordering. 34 tests pass, clippy clean.

reconstruct() now builds per-page block vecs and runs a merge_page_breaks pass. Merge fires only when BOTH boundary blocks are Paragraphs (so a Heading/Table/Figure/List boundary blocks it automatically), the leading paragraph starts lowercase, AND the trailing one does not end in .!?. Hyphen repair reuses the existing in-page append_line logic. 7 tests added incl. negative cases.

Added is_full_width_line (per-CELL straddle of the midpoint gutter, or a single cell >0.66 page width -- deliberately per-cell, not per-line-extent, to avoid the false positive where two unrelated column fragments share a baseline) and split_two_column_page_into_bands, which walks the page top-to-bottom and flushes left/right accumulators whenever full-width-ness changes. Left-then-right order preserved WITHIN each band; full-width elements emitted at their correct vertical position between bands. Single-column fast path untouched. 2 tests added incl. the no-full-width negative case.

TextSpan now carries font_name (real PostScript BaseFont name) + FontStyle{family, bold: Option<bool>, italic: Option<bool>} -- Option so 'unknown' is distinct from 'known not bold'. Derivation prefers the authoritative FontDescriptor (/Flags bit 7 italic, /FontWeight>=600, ForceBold bit 19, /StemV>=140) and falls back to PostScript name heuristics (Bold/Black/Semibold/-Bd/Ital/Oblique, glued suffixes, URW/Nimbus 4-letter abbreviations). Subset prefix (ABCDEF+) stripped for family only. Implemented via an independent lopdf content-stream walk (Tf/Tj/TJ/q/Q/Do) queued per text-showing operator and popped per pdf-extract begin_word -- degrades to None rather than guessing when the queue runs dry. lopdf promoted to a direct dep (already transitive via pdf-extract, same 0.42 line, so Cargo unifies -- no new C surface). 22 tests. Cross-crate TextSpan literal breakage in kopitiam-document fixed with ..TextSpan::default().

Naming resolved (AID-0001): no kopitiam-cli/kopitiam-tui crates -- the runtime is an engine, exposed via the existing apps/cli; kopitiam-core repurposed from the misnamed 'core' stub into the runtime's shared vocabulary; crates added incrementally rather than 17 empty stubs up front. kopitiam-core/tensor/loader/tokenizer/runtime registered in workspace.dependencies and building in release.

3-tier IconSet (Nerd/Unicode/Ascii, defaulting to Unicode when unsure), 2.4MB JetBrains Mono Nerd Font embedded via include_bytes! with its OFL-1.1 licence, 'kvim --install-font' writing ~/.termux/font.ttf on Termux (detected via PREFIX, not cfg!(target_os=android) -- the platform being Android doesn't tell you the TERMINAL is Termux). kvim binary is 4.0MB, well under the 10MB budget. 10 tests.

Resolved by AID-0005. Maintainer supplied the key fact: they run cargo on Android. That reframes it -- rust-analyzer and texlab are Rust programs, so cargo install works there even though NO upstream publishes an aarch64-linux-android binary. Source is now a 4-tier ladder: OnPath (covers both their desktop and Termux 'pkg install', with zero network) -> SystemPackage (Termux pkg -- NOT GitHub releases, the source AID-0003 missed) -> CargoInstall (Rust servers; rustup component preferred over a slow source build) -> Download (desktop only) -> honest Unavailable. Tests pin that Android NEVER resolves to a download (any URL there would be fabricated) and that a desktop with a real download is never sent to compile from source. REMAINING GAP: lua-language-server is C++/LuaJIT, no crate to build -- Termux pkg is its only Android tier.

Rope-backed Buffer with a real undo TREE (branching, not a stack), marks that shift with edits, CRLF preservation, grapheme-indexed positions. 49 tests incl. a 1000-iteration property test against a naive String reference.

Operator-pending state machine (register/count1/operator/count2 accumulators + State enum for multi-key tokens). All motions, operators, text objects, registers with granularity, counts, dot-repeat, macros, ~15 ex commands. 62 tests. Note:  at idle is hop (the maintainer's config remaps it); find-char remains reachable composed with an operator (df(), pinned by a test.

All 20 Lua plugins reimplemented natively: telescope->nucleo picker, neo-tree->lazy filetree, harpoon, hop (prefix-free labels), easy-align, git status (parses .git/HEAD + index binary format, no gix). lazy.nvim deleted -- nothing to install when plugins are compiled in. 44 tests. NOT YET WIRED INTO THE UI: the engines are done and tested but EditorResponse::Action currently reports 'not wired into the UI yet' -- presenting pickers/tree/hop overlays is the remaining UI work.

[[bin]] kvim ships from the kopitiam-neovim crate. Config::default() IS the maintainer's ported ~/.config/nvim (settings, keymaps, leader=Space, gruvbox, 3 LSP servers). Verified end-to-end through a PTY: dd deletes a line, A!!!<Esc> appends, :wq writes to disk. 6.4MB binary.

Fixed. New position.rs models PositionEncoding{Utf8,Utf16,Utf32} with char<->wire-unit conversion using real line text (never raw arithmetic on the two numbers). LspClient now advertises all three encodings and HONOURS whatever the server negotiates, instead of assuming utf-8==char offsets; require_utf8_positions (which refused non-utf-8 servers) is gone. edit.rs and session.rs thread the encoding through both directions. Tested against ASCII/CJK/astral-plane emoji (UTF-16 surrogate pair)/ZWJ family (5 chars, 8 UTF-16 units)/combining vs precomposed accents, incl. a named regression test: rename target sitting AFTER multi-byte text under utf-8 encoding. 21 tests, including a LIVE rust-analyzer integration test that ran for real and passes. Clippy clean.

Phase 1 complete. LocalAdapter in kopitiam-ai now wraps kopitiam-runtime's on-CPU Qwen engine behind the ModelAdapter trait (default-on  cargo feature, so a cloud-only consumer never compiles a tensor engine). ChatML template verified against llama.cpp's OWN test fixtures rather than a self-authored one. EchoAdapter retained so kopitiam-workflow stays testable with no weights. 19 tests (+2 with --no-default-features). This closes the Offline First chain: KOPITIAM can now run a local model with no network and no tokens. NOTE: no real Qwen GGUF exists on this machine, so end-to-end is proven against a synthetic GGUF built byte-by-byte in-test, plus an #[ignore]d test ready for a real model.

Done — src/lsp/ (55 tests). registry (server->platform resolution), install (plan/fetch), position (grapheme<->UTF-8/16/32, tested against astral-plane + ZWJ emoji), client (wraps kopitiam-semantic's rust-analyzer session), completion (LSP+buffer+path merged and ranked). Mason is gone: no npm/pip/go anywhere. Android acquisition solved by the four-tier ladder in AID-0005 (PATH -> Termux pkg -> cargo/rustup -> prebuilt download -> honest no), closed as kopitiam-cj0.9. NOTE: definition/references still return Unsupported because kopitiam-semantic exposes no generic LSP request pass-through -- that gap is real and is tracked by kopitiam-cj0.10's UI-wiring work.

Phase 2 substantially done.

STOCHASTIC SAMPLING (29 tests): temperature, top-k, top-p, min-p, repetition penalty, composed as a PIPELINE of logit transforms rather than branches. Hand-rolled seedable xorshift PRNG (~20 lines, no rand dep) -- seedable is mandatory, since CLAUDE.md requires determinism and an unseedable sampler is untestable. Two tests guard the classic silent failures: top_p_includes_the_crossing_token (the nucleus off-by-one -- excluding it quietly narrows the nucleus and the model just gets duller with nothing to point at) and temperature_zero_degrades_exactly_to_greedy (so the two paths can never silently disagree).

QUANTIZED MATMUL: fused integer kernel consuming Q4_0/Q8_0 weight blocks DIRECTLY, quantizing the activation to Q8_0 on the fly and accumulating in i32. The dequantize-then-f32 path is KEPT as the reference the fast path is gated against -- they must AGREE, not merely both look plausible.

MEASURED (bench_quantized_vs_f32_weights, #[ignore]d):
  model size  9,982,144 B -> 3,051,712 B   3.27x smaller
  prefill      765 -> 792 tok/s            ~flat (compute-bound)
  decode        41 -> 193 tok/s            4.7x faster (memory-bandwidth-bound)
The prefill/decode split matches theory exactly, which is why it is trustworthy.

TWO TEST-FIXTURE BUGS FOUND AND PINNED (both would be catastrophic in a kernel):
  - Q4_0's zero-point is 8: nibble 0 decodes to -8*scale, NOT zero.
  - Q8_0's step is amax/127: integers are exact ONLY when amax is exactly 127.

NOT DONE (deliberately): the execution graph / scheduler. An inference forward pass is a fixed sequence of ops; a general graph engine buys little until there is operator fusion to schedule. Deferred to Phase 3 where it belongs, alongside SIMD. Filed as remaining scope on kopitiam-082.5.

kopitiam-runtime 95 tests, kopitiam-tensor 105 tests, clippy clean.

kopitiam-insurance scaffolded: provenance model, clauses, definitions+resolution, exclusions+write-backs, schedule/benefit tables, endorsement overrides, cross-references, classification, anomalies, ontology emission. 100 tests green, clippy clean, workspace builds.
Duplicate of kopitiam-bjo, which already tracks this crate.

Fixed: App::render_windows now renders each leaf from its window.buffer via EditorHost::buffer_by_id, with the active window's live cursor and inactive windows' saved cursors. Regression test vsplit_opening_a_second_file_shows_two_different_buffers_side_by_side asserts the painted cells of the two panes differ. Verified through the real binary over a PTY.

Fixed: WindowTree gained focus_direction (spatial, using laid-out rects), cycle/focus_prev, and App intercepts <C-w> in Normal mode (handle_window_key). Full set: h/j/k/l w/W p s/v/n c/q o = +/-/</> x r. Tests ctrl_w_l_moves_focus_to_the_right_pane and each_window_keeps_its_own_cursor_across_focus_switches. Getting back INTO the file tree via <C-w>h from the buffer is still e-twice (the tree is an overlay, not a tree leaf per AID-0018) — noted for a follow-up but not blocking.

Hover popup now anchors at the cursor via shared anchored_rect (Anchor::Above), reusing the completion menu's edge-flip/clamp logic. 7 new cell-asserting tests + PTY-verified against live rust-analyzer (cursor col8/row7 -> box top-left col8/row8, column-aligned & adjacent). Wired git branch + dirty marker into kvim statusline from plugins::git::status(). Pure .git/HEAD read (ref/detached/worktree-file/no-repo), cached & refreshed on idle tick (1s throttle) + on buffer-dir change, never per frame. Nerd-font glyph or git: fallback, gruvbox aqua. Tests green, clippy clean, PTY-verified (git:main*). Commit 2ecadde.

Plugin engines all wired into the UI: file tree (<leader>e), hop (f), fuzzy pickers \ff/\fb/\fh (cj0.10 pickers), harpoon <leader>b/<Esc>/q (cj0.10.7), git branch in statusline (cj0.10.8), and LSP gd/gr/rn/hover. Remaining sub-tasks (cj0.10.1 filetree engine bug, .4/.5/.6 term/edge-move/tabs, .9/.10 harpoon persistence/reorder) tracked separately.

Landed: ]p/[p/[P/]P reindent linewise register to current line indent (PUT_FIXINDENT); charwise = plain put.

Landed: = operator with deterministic brace-depth reindent fallback (==, ={motion}, visual =). LSP rangeFormatting deferred to cj0.57 (needs kopitiam-semantic method, out of owned dir); real indenter cj0.58.
